items.summary: An ABC NEWS Verify investigation raises questions about popular independent fuel security dashboards that have emerged online, including one whose creators are linked to the Liberal Party.

items.title: Mixed reaction to Chinese-backed company's purchase of Kestrel Coal Mine
items.summary: Yancoal Australia will acquire a majority stake in what it says is the "largest producing" underground coal mine in the country. The announcement has been met with a mixed reaction.

items.title: Newmont's Cadia gold mine suspends underground operations after earthquake in Orange
items.summary: All underground workers at Newmont's Cadia gold mine have been brought to the surface as inspections are carried out following an earthquake.

items.title: Platypus spotted outside hotel in Marian, Queensland
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Anti-war protester to face court over 'From the River to the Sea' banner
items.summary: Jim Dowling was arrested outside Boeing's headquarters in Brisbane earlier this year.

items.title: Nathan River Resources stands down workers with no pay, blames fuel crisis
items.summary: A Northern Territory mine has stood down workers and stopped operations, blaming the high price of fuel. Unions say that's "rubbish".

items.title: Jury retires to consider verdict in Irma Palasics murder trial after weeks of evidence
items.summary: Justice David Mossop told the jury it had a "complex" and "substantial" task after the court was presented with 53 witnesses, 14 statements or recordings, and 97 exhibits.

items.title: What is financial stress and how does it affect our decisions?
items.summary: If financial stress is causing distractions — for example, you're worrying about money when you should be concentrating on driving or work — there might be something deeper happening.

items.title: Interest in novated leases for electric vehicles soaring in Australia amid fuel crisis
items.summary: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering getting an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: Kiera Austin creates period shorts, Kate Moloney collision raises safety questions and Mary Cholhok must lift
items.summary: World Cup-winning Diamond Kiera Austin remembers having to learn to use a tampon earlier than she was ready in order to play netball. Now she's developed a period undershort she hopes will address the alarming rates of Australian teens dropping out of sport.

items.title: Federal government urges rise in minimum wage above rate of inflation
items.summary: In its submission to the Fair Work Commission's annual wage review, the government again calls for the lowest-paid workers to not go backwards.

items.title: Japan urges public not to panic-buy toilet paper amid Iran war
items.summary: Japan's government releases a statement assuring people there is no issue with supply and no need to stock up, despite "reports and social media posts regarding the distribution of toilet paper".

items.title: Albanese hopeful about end of war against Iran as budget looms
items.summary: Perhaps the prime minister was hoping US President Donald Trump was tuning in to Triple M when he claimed that the attack on Iran had now achieved its aims, and offered an off-ramp for the war to end.

items.title: Raissa Butkowski to contest Farrer for Liberals after Sussan Ley's resignation
items.summary: Raissa Butkowski has been selected as the Liberal candidate for the seat of Farrer.

items.title: Former spy boss Dennis Richardson resigns from antisemitism royal commission
items.summary: Dennis Richardson was originally tapped by Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in the weeks after the Bondi terror attack to lead a review into the federal government's intelligence and security agencies.

items.title: Australian designer Katie Perry wins trademark case against popstar Katy Perry in High Court
items.summary: Australian fashion designer Katie Perry wins her High Court battle against international pop star Katy Perry in a long-running trademark case.

items.title: Nationals select former military commander Brad Robertson to contest Farrer by-election
items.summary: The National Party's candidate for the Farrer by-election, Brad Robertson, says the people of Farrer have been "waiting" for the Nationals to come back.

items.title: Labor on track to win Nightcliff by-election, taking NT's single Greens seat
items.summary: Labor is on track to win the Nightcliff by-election, taking the Greens' first and only seat held in the Northern Territory parliament.

items.title: Forensic pathologist cannot determine Irma Palasics cause of death as trial continues
items.summary: The ACT Supreme Court hears evidence in the trial of two Melbourne men who are accused of murdering Irma Palasics in her home in 1999.

items.title: WA Corruption and Crime Commission left itself open to corruption risk for years, report finds
items.summary: Flaws in the Corruption and Crime Commission's finance systems left it vulnerable to "fictitious or fraudulent transactions" for at least three years, according to WA's financial scrutineer.

items.title: Federal government's plan for Darwin Port criticised by former NT treasurer Dave Tollner
items.summary: Former NT treasurer Dave Tollner describes the federal government's plans to take back the 99-year lease of Darwin Port as "an appalling waste of money".

items.title: The Nightcliff by-election is this weekend. Here is why it's happening and how to vote
items.summary: Residents living in Darwin's northern suburbs of Nightcliff, Rapid Creek and parts of Coconut Grove will be heading to the polls this Saturday to vote in a by-election. It comes after the former member for Nightcliff resigned in February.

items.title: The doctor fighting to get the families of ISIS fighters out of Syria
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Reawakening
items.summary: The Bondi Beach terrorist attack signalled the resurgence of the Islamic State terrorist group in Australia. For the past two years, ABC Investigations has tracked the group's revival, identifying key figures, mapping the network and raising questions about the response of police, intelligence agencies and governments.

items.title: Disenfranchised Farrer voters turn out in droves to consider One Nation
items.summary: This southern New South Wales town of Gerogery is typically dead on a Monday night, but that all changed when One Nation came to town.

items.title: Man accused of murdering Canberra grandmother Irma Palasics pleads guilty to burglary charge as trial begins
items.summary: Lawyers for one of two men on trial for the 1999 murder of Canberra grandmother Irma Palasics tell the ACT Supreme Court he was just the lookout, and had nothing to do with her death.

items.title: Australian women and children with links to ISIS forced to head back to camp in Syria
items.summary: A group of Australian families with links to Islamic State fighters are forced to turn around after leaving a camp in Syria to try and begin their journey home to Australia.

items.title: Two Chinese nationals accused of foreign interference granted bail in ACT Magistrates Court
items.summary: Police allege the two Chinese nationals, who are charged with reckless foreign interference for alleged spying on the Canberra branch of a Buddhist association, are linked to a woman arrested on the same charge last year.

items.title: Bondi Beach terrorist Sajid Akram voiced support for Islamic State in 2019, former ASIO agent says
items.summary: Bondi Beach terrorist Sajid Akram voiced support for the Islamic State group (IS) in 2019 and expressed admiration for a leading Al Qaeda propagandist, according to accounts from two men who moved in the same extremist circles.

items.title: Native title holders take appeal over one of Australia's largest groundwater licences to High Court
items.summary: The cultural value of water will be front and centre in the High Court in Canberra today as a group of native title holders from Central Australia fight to assert cultural rights over an aquifer under their lands.

items.title: High Court to consider if Victoria's strict cap on individual political donations is discriminatory
items.summary: Two independent Victorian political candidates will ask the High Court to overturn a state law capping political donations to just under $5,000 but allowing an exception for the major parties.

items.title: Chinese ambassador hints at retaliation if Port of Darwin is returned to Australian control
items.summary: China's ambassador to Australia, Xiao Qian, questions the Albanese government's ethics in wanting to return the Port of Darwin to Australian hands.

items.title: Trump threatens to invoke Insurrection Act over Minnesota anti-ICE protests
items.summary: Such a move would allow the president to deploy troops to quell persistent protests in the city of Minneapolis, where two shootings connected to immigration crackdowns are fuelling public anger.

items.title: Iranian Australians wary of foreign intervention or regime change amid fear for relatives
items.summary: Some members of the Iranian diaspora in Australia are wary of foreign intervention in Iran, but others say there may not be another choice.

items.title: This toad busting group is determined to take back Magnetic Island
items.summary: On a tropical Queensland island, 'toad busters' have decided the cane toad's spread across northern Australia should not be a lost cause.

items.title: LNP's Wayde Chiesa promises to get to work after victory in Hinchinbrook by-election
items.summary: The LNP's Wayde Chiesa will be the next representative for the Queensland seat of Hinchinbrook, which had been safely held by Katter's Australian Party since 2017.

items.title: TasNetworks says 'large' executive payouts are due to legacy entitlements that no longer exist
items.summary: Tasmania's state-owned electricity distributor says a wave of executives receiving six-figure termination payments will, in the end, save the utility money in the long run.

items.title: SA's ICAC 'busier than ever' despite watered down powers
items.summary: South Australia's Independent Commissioner Against Corruption (ICAC) Emma Townsend has taken over an anti-corruption commission described by her predecessor as the "weakest integrity agency in Australia". Does she agree?

items.title: Eight candidates running for Townsville mayor at postal by-election
items.summary: Townsville residents will select from a field of nine candidates at a mayoral by-election next month.

items.title: The Assembly's former students return to work on season two with Leigh Sales
items.summary: Aspiring journalism students with autism are back to grill some of Australia's most prominent figures, and this time they've got seasoned peers behind the camera cheering them on.

items.title: Scenic Rim's Summer Land farm aims to make camel meat, milk mainstream
items.summary: A Scenic Rim farmer is trying to build a livestock industry around camels, but says one of the biggest challenges will be changing public opinion about the feral pests.

items.title: No Return: Australia's Missing Billions
items.summary: Four Corners is the home of Australian investigative journalism.

items.title: Trading in Chaos
items.summary: Four Corners is the home of Australian investigative journalism.

items.title: Heather Ewart reflects on the tough road to Back Roads success
items.summary: Heather Ewart was a powerhouse political reporter when she was asked to create a show about regional Australia. A decade later, and with her final season going to air, Ewart admits that getting the idea off the ground was no easy feat.

items.title: Port Pirie and Hobart smelters need 'urgent' government help, owner says
items.summary: The owner of the Port Pirie and Hobart smelters says its operations will "simply not survive" without taxpayer support to combat alleged market distortion from China.

items.title: China didn't want to be part of Australia's election story — but behind the silence was a loud message
items.summary: Xi Jinping sees Australia as a strategic partner — a relatively stable player amid the turbulence of Beijing's deteriorating relationship with the United States.

items.title: Health, climate and community sport: What older voters in the Tasmanian seat of Bass are talking about this election
items.summary: The federal seat of Bass is held by a margin of just 1.4 per cent. For these older voters who live in the electorate, health, climate and community sport are the issues that will decide their vote.

items.title: Mobile coverage is an election issue in regional areas. What's been promised?
items.summary: For many regional and remote Australians, reliable mobile coverage can be life-saving. They are desperate for both sides of politics to take meaningful action towards eliminating black spots.

items.title: Tiwi Islanders eager to have their say as remote voting for the federal election begins
items.summary: Voters in the small remote community of Pirlangimpi on the Tiwi Islands, north of Darwin, have become some of the first to cast their votes for the federal election.

items.title: Energy projects in South West WA threaten votes for major federal parties
items.summary: Australia's two major parties have tied their federal election fates to controversial energy projects in WA's South West where the issues have polarised voters.

items.title: Cost of living, housing among key concerns in SA marginal seat of Boothby
items.summary: At this year’s federal election, the South Australian seat of Boothby is one of the state’s two marginal seats. As part of the ABC’s “Your Say” campaign, we spoke to residents in the electorate about their biggest concerns.

items.title: Melbourne seat of Calwell highlights Labor woes as Gaza pain galvanises Muslim voters
items.summary: Dissatisfaction with the Albanese government's stance on Israel-Palestine among many Arab and Muslim voters threatens to unseat Labor MPs, in some places for the first time.

items.title: Next-generation voters call for better engagement ahead of federal election
items.summary: Young people in Albury say issues that directly affect them and their communities are not being addressed by major parties for the 2025 election campaign.

items.title: Graduates in 'debt vortex' as HECS front and centre for first time in an election
items.summary: New research shows a sharp rise in HECS debts, despite graduate earning power declining, as student loans become a potent election issue for the first time.

items.title: Australia's growing cohort of tenants fear they will rent forever
items.summary: Home ownership rates in Australia have been falling for decades, and with rental vacancies at record lows and prices at historic highs, many fear their dream of owning a home may be out of reach. Some experts say rental caps could help.

items.title: Australians tell ABC's Your Say they are struggling more than ever with housing costs
items.summary: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they are struggling more than ever with housing costs.

items.title: Push for seniors' dental scheme grows ahead of federal election
items.summary: Imagine living in aged care and not having your teeth or dentures properly cleaned and checked each day. That's a risk faced by elderly Australians, prompting calls for the next federal government to boost oral health training and services.

items.title: Voters tell ABC's Your Say they want politicians with a vision, not bickering
items.summary: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they want courage and vision from politicians, not bickering.

items.title: Have Your Say: What was the issue, policy or event that decided your vote?
items.summary: After a five-week campaign, the results are in with Labor returned to government. Is there a policy or issue that swayed your vote? Have Your Say.

items.title: Reserve Bank restructure closer to reality after Treasurer Jim Chalmers offers olive branch to Angus Taylor
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia is a step closer to its biggest restructure in three decades after the treasurer offers an assurance to his Coalition counterpart.

items.title: The US election is about style more than substance. It's why Kamala Harris needs to nail her DNC speech in about eight seconds
items.summary: When the Democrat presidential candidate Kamala Harris delivers her speech to the Democratic National Convention today, style will be almost everything.

items.title: Call for button battery redesign after new study reveals horrific injuries and fatalities
items.summary: A new study reveals the horrific injuries suffered after swallowing or inserting button batteries but manufacturers aren't prioritising their redesign.

items.title: We reviewed the health habits of centenarians. These four things could lead to a longer life
items.summary: People are living longer, and we can expect to see more people reach 100 in the years to come. But what sort of factors specifically contribute to living to 100?

items.title: The ABC and Four Corners investigated Rupert Murdoch many times. Here's what we found
items.summary: With a career spanning 70 years, Rupert Murdoch is no stranger to facing the same kind of interviewing and investigative treatment that he has dished out in his media outlets.

items.title: Update your ABC News homepage for more local stories
items.summary: We've got an ABC News homepage for you. Adjust your settings for the right mix of local and national news.

items.title: Peter Slipper sentenced: Former speaker gets 300 hours of community service on dishonesty charges
items.summary: Former federal parliamentary speaker Peter Slipper has been ordered to complete 300 hours of community service after being sentenced for misusing his Cabcharge allowance to visit Canberra wineries in 2010.
